title: Record: 11L + Hadamard Rotation + VE128 + cuDNN SDPA (val_bpb: 1.1365)
body:
## Summary
- First application of Walsh-Hadamard rotation for int6 quantization in this competition
- Improves zstd compression from 1.70x to 1.76x, recovering 530KB of artifact headroom
- Headroom directly enables Shared Value Embeddings (VE128) which previously overflowed
- CPU parameter probe guided hyperparameter selection, reducing GPU compute by ~84%
- 3-seed mean: 1.1365 +/- 0.0005 BPB. All artifacts under 16MB.

## Key Findings
- Hadamard rotation and GPTQ are substitutes, not complements, at int6 precision
- Late QAT (STE fake-quantization) was dead code in all prior work due to instance vs class attribute shadowing
- FlashAttention 3 compresses 1.8% worse than cuDNN SDPA with zstd-22
- Removing dead QAT guard from CastedLinear.forward() yields 7% throughput gain via torch.compile guard elimination

## Test plan
- [x] 3-seed validation (1337, 42, 2024) with mean 1.1365, std 0.0005
- [x] All artifacts under 16,000,000 bytes
- [x] All training runs under 600s wallclock on 8xH100
- [x] No network calls during evaluation
- [x] Artifact is self-contained

Erick Aleman | EA Cognitive | www.eacognitive.com | github.com/eacognitive
